commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c0ce21c37753db383b5beaf4927a750d688b4963)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `StatusItemController+Menu.swift` so the Codex visible-account refresh task weakly captures the controller and menu before refreshing account-scoped state.

Reproducibility: yes. for the patch defect by source inspection: the PR adds weak captures but then immediately `guard let self` before awaiting the account-scoped refresh. I did not run a live memory-lifetime probe.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The blocker combines a code fix with missing contributor-side runtime proof, so this should stay in normal PR review rather than queue an automated repair.

target.title: Avoid retaining menu refresh state
target.body:
## Summary
- Avoid retaining `StatusItemController` and the open menu for the full Codex account-scoped refresh task.
- Keep the existing phase-change callback guarded by weak captures so menu refreshes only run while the menu/controller still exist.

## Proof
Focused proof rerun locally on June 16, 2026 PT at head `c0ce21c3`.

`swiftlint --strict Sources/CodexBar/StatusItemController+Menu.swift`:

```text
Linting Swift files at paths Sources/CodexBar/StatusItemController+Menu.swift
Linting 'StatusItemController+Menu.swift' (1/1)
Done linting! Found 0 violations, 0 serious in 1 file.
```

`swift build --build-tests 2>&1 | tee /tmp/codexbar-pr1571-build.log`:

```text
Build complete! (123.24 sec)
```

Strong-capture diagnostic scan against the captured build log:

```text
$ rg -n "ImplicitStrongCapture|weak ownership of capture" /tmp/codexbar-pr1571-build.log
# no matches
No implicit strong-capture diagnostics found.
```

CI is also green on the PR:

```text
lint-build-test: success
build-linux-cli (linux-x64, ubuntu-24.04): success
build-linux-cli (linux-arm64, ubuntu-24.04-arm): success
GitGuardian Security Checks: success
```

## Notes
- This is intentionally separate from the logging performance PR.
target.author: prospectore
